title: Immediate consequences & lethal rash-action tolerance
summary: Attack a cop → instant cuff + backup. Grab or shoot the gun → death. Waiting while detained forces backup arrival. System/diverge banners centered.
changes: Server injections: AUTHORITY RESPONSE (1st assault), LETHAL CONSEQUENCE (gun/shoot/burn), DETENTION TIMER (wait stasis)
Backup must arrive within 1-2 turns; no 10-turn arrest loops
Strip leaked [COMBAT ESCALATION] and [SCENE continues] from visible text
System and diverge log entries centered in terminal
Engine version bump to v5.8

title: Combat escalation — guards fight back, no plot nudges mid-fight
summary: Server injects a mandatory fight-resolution block when you keep attacking a passive NPC. No more smoke/murmur hooks during brawls.
changes: [COMBAT ESCALATION — server authoritative] fires after sustained assault or passive guard loops
Banned passive fight phrases; trained authority must win by takedown/KO/relocation
Active combat: zero ambient nudges, no pivot to mysteries when player asks mid-fight
Debug panel: Combat escalation (next turn) section
Engine version bump to v5.7

title: NPC registry & realistic combat agency
summary: Every person you meet gets a tracked persona in STATE. NPCs fight back — trained guards win fights instead of passively absorbing punches.
changes: characters[] expanded: personality, memory[], disposition, combat_posture, training, authority_level
Rule: any human in [SCENE] gets a sheet same turn (plot cast or passerby)
NPC COMBAT & SELF-DEFENSE: no passive punching bags; authority wins vs untrained player
Realistic outcomes: restraint, knockout, relocation, lethal force, hard END
Debug panel: Characters overview + JSON roster + prompt excerpts
Engine version bump to v5.6

title: Patch notes grouped by day
summary: Patch notes now roll up under a calendar-day heading, sorted by significance within each day.
changes: /patch-notes groups updates by date (newest day first)
Within a day: significance 1–100, highest at top
content/README.md documents the convention for future entries

title: Abstract wake-up lines
summary: Openings are generic place labels only — no sensory detail until the player acts.
changes: Opening: YOU WAKE UP IN/ON [GENERIC PLACE] — no adjectives, lighting, materials, mood
Bad example blocked: dimly lit cramped quarters with metal walls
Full sensory truth lives in STATE; revealed when player looks, moves, or asks
Engine version bump to v5.5

title: Grounded GTA-style worlds & plain openings
summary: Seed dials bias toward contemporary normal settings. Openings use simple place names — forest, street, motel — not fairy tale prose.
changes: Weighted seed dials: ~77% grounded world_type, street-level place grain, low tone
Prompt: NORMAL WORLD DEFAULT — GTA-style open-world energy, mundane locations
Openings: YOU WAKE UP IN/ON [SIMPLE PLACE] — no magic, no lore dump
WorldSpec constraints reinforce contemporary settings when grounded
Typing sound stops when display catches up; syncing label while hidden STATE streams
Engine version bump to v5.4

title: Conditions, story debug & hybrid randomness
summary: Broad condition families tick every turn. Server rolls random table+tier; model narrates. Full story and randomness observability in debug.
changes: 7 condition kinds (trauma, exposure, toxicity, asphyxia, exhaustion, distress, restraint)
Hybrid randomness: server roll injected per player turn; hazard/discovery/social/ambient tiers
chaos baseline derived from seed tone + npc_agency
Debug: stories, conditions, random roll, LLM payload sections
Engine version bump to v5.3

title: Seed dials, WorldSpec & discoverable laws
summary: Each digit of the seed code biases world physics and social physics. Worlds seed discoverable laws; share links still store the exact turn-1 bible.
changes: 10-axis WorldSpec decoder (type, place, isolation, law, opacity, agency, scarcity, rules, stickiness, tone)
Opening generation injects WORLDSPEC constraints from the dial code
STATE laws[] — surface vs true_rule; players earn knowledge through consequence
Crossed pressures, NPC rule carriers, threads as law probes, consistency audits in prompt
Share flow: digits bias create; preferred dial code saved; load still exact bible
Debug panel: WorldSpec dials + laws slices
Engine version bump to v5.0

title: Structured state, combat realism & living world
summary: Engine v4.0: JSON character/inventory/body stats, hard capability ceilings, soft endings so the world continues after the starting plot, heat, locations, and open-world chill.
changes: STATE JSON every turn: player + NPCs with fixed 0-100 core stats, body map, inventory
Stats generated when characters appear and updated in the background from interactions
Hard capability ceilings — plot cannot override physics; no invented kung fu
Body-part injuries gate sprint, aim, and grapple
Realistic multi-opponent combat and heat/wanted escalation
Soft SOFT_END keeps the prompt open after starting-plot resolution; hard END for death only
Location graph + chill exploration; multi-phase plots with aftermath arcs
Declared abilities for heightened/fantastical worlds only

title: Opening lines stay in character POV
summary: Wake-up scenes only say what you could notice on the spot — hidden geography and plot truth stay in STATE until you discover them.
changes: Opening: YOU WAKE UP IN [immediate place] only — no 'buried under a city' spoilers
Hedge uncertain impressions (seemingly abandoned) instead of omniscient labels
Character-POV rule applies to all [SCENE] text, not just the opening
Engine version bump to v4.2

title: Starting plot, not main plot
summary: Renames the seeded tension to starting_plot and tells the engine not to railroad players back onto it.
changes: STATE key main_plot → starting_plot; active_track default starting
Soft-end label STARTING PLOT RESOLVED (never MAIN PLOT)
Explicit no-railroad rules: chill exploration is first-class; ambient nudges are not plot magnets
Engine version bump to v4.1 (invalidates v4.0 local saves)

title: Rate limits & seed RPC lockdown
summary: Hardened the public API so the model key and shared-world table are harder to abuse.
changes: Per-IP rate limit on /api/game (30/min) with 429 + Retry-After
Per-IP rate limit on seed create (10/min)
Seed RPCs executable only via service_role; anon REST returns 401
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY required in production

title: Response typing sound
summary: A looping typewriter SFX plays while engine responses reveal character-by-character during streaming and seed load.
changes: Looping typing sound during typewriter reveal (stream + seed/retry)
public/sounds/response_typing_sound.wav + lib/typingSound.ts
Stops when on-screen text catches up; syncing label while hidden STATE streams

title: Public patch notes page
summary: Devlog lives in-repo and renders at /patch-notes.
changes: New /patch-notes route (CRT styled)
content/devlog.json is the source of truth — append to publish
Links from the terminal and gallery

title: Branching story, consequences & endings
summary: Engine v3.3: multi-thread worlds, personas, divergence indicator, lethal combat, rewind/retry.
changes: Extended [WORLD] with MAIN_PLOT, THREADS, CONSEQUENCES, CHARACTERS, END_CLAUSES
YOUR STORY IS CHANGING divergence line
Multiple end clauses including death
Post-end rewind, retry this world, new world

title: Local session save & resume
summary: Close the tab, crash, or come back later — progress and world seed restore from localStorage.
changes: Auto-save history, terminal log, seed code, and end state to localStorage
Auto-resume on / when a valid save exists (engine-version gated)
Mid-stream recovery if you quit while waiting for a reply
New world clears the local save

title: Shared world seeds & gallery
summary: Share a turn-1 world as a numeric seed. Others load the same opening and hidden bible.
changes: Share world button copies /s/CODE link
Deep links and most-played gallery
Supabase-backed worlds table with hardened create_world RPC
